heading: University transportation centers program

children.heading: .—Establishment and operation
children.label: 1 .—Establishment and operation
children.text: The Secretary of Transportation, acting through the Assistant Secretary for Research and Technology (referred to in this section as the “Secretary”), shall make grants under this section to eligible nonprofit institutions of higher education to establish and operate university transportation centers.

children.label: A
children.text: to advance transportation expertise and technology in the varied disciplines that comprise the field of transportation through education, research, and technology transfer activities;

children.label: B
children.text: to provide for a critical multimodal transportation knowledge base outside of the Department of Transportation; and

children.label: C
children.text: to address critical workforce needs and educate the next generation of transportation leaders with respect to the matters described in subparagraphs (A) through (G) of section 6503(c)(1).

children.heading: .—Applications
children.label: 1 .—Applications
children.text: To receive a grant under this section, a consortium of nonprofit institutions of higher education shall submit to the Secretary an application that is in such form and contains such information as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: .—Limitation
children.label: A .—Limitation
children.text: A lead institution of a consortium of nonprofit institutions of higher education, as applicable, may only receive 1 grant per fiscal year as a lead institution under this section, except as provided in subparagraph (B).

children.heading: .—Exception for consortium members that are not lead institutions
children.label: B .—Exception for consortium members that are not lead institutions
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to a nonprofit institution of higher education that is a member of a consortium of nonprofit institutions of higher education but not the lead institution of such consortium.

children.heading: .—Coordination
children.label: 3 .—Coordination
children.text: The Secretary shall solicit grant applications for national transportation centers, regional transportation centers, and Tier 1 university transportation centers with identical advertisement schedules and deadlines.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Except as otherwise provided by this section, the Secretary shall award grants under this section in nonexclusive candidate topic areas established by the Secretary that address the research priorities described in subparagraphs (A) through (G) of section 6503(c)(1).

children.label: i
children.text: the demonstrated ability of the recipient to address each specific topic area described in the research and strategic plans of the recipient;

children.label: ii
children.text: the demonstrated research, technology transfer, and education resources available to the recipient to carry out this section;

children.label: iii
children.text: the ability of the recipient to provide leadership in solving immediate and long-range national and regional transportation problems;

children.label: iv
children.text: the ability of the recipient to carry out research, education, and technology transfer activities that are multimodal and multidisciplinary in scope;

children.label: I
children.text: degree-granting programs or programs that provide other industry-recognized credentials; and

children.label: II
children.text: outreach activities to attract new entrants into the transportation field, including women and underrepresented populations;

children.label: vi
children.text: the demonstrated ability of the recipient to disseminate results and spur the implementation of transportation research and education programs through national or statewide continuing education programs;

children.label: vii
children.text: the demonstrated commitment of the recipient to the use of peer review principles and other research best practices in the selection, management, and dissemination of research projects;

children.label: viii
children.text: the strategic plan submitted by the recipient describing the proposed research to be carried out by the recipient and the performance metrics to be used in assessing the performance of the recipient in meeting the stated research, technology transfer, education, and outreach goals; and

children.label: ix
children.text: the ability of the recipient to implement the proposed program in a cost-efficient manner, such as through cost sharing and overall reduced overhead, facilities, and administrative costs.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall provide to each applicant, upon request, any materials, including copies of reviews (with any information that would identify a reviewer redacted), used in the evaluation process of the proposal of the applicant.

children.label: i
children.text: specific criteria of evaluation used in the review;

children.label: ii
children.text: descriptions of the review process; and

children.label: iii
children.text: explanations of the selected awards.

children.heading: .—Outside stakeholders
children.label: 6 .—Outside stakeholders
children.text: The Secretary shall, to the maximum extent practicable, consult external stakeholders, including the Transportation Research Board of the National Research Council of the National Academies, to evaluate and competitively review all proposals.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Not later than 1 year after the date of enactment of this section, the Secretary shall select grant recipients under subsection (b) and make grant amounts available to the selected recipients.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), the Secretary shall provide grants to 5 consortia that the Secretary determines best meet the criteria described in subsection (b)(4).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: For each fiscal year, a grant made available under this paragraph shall be not greater than $4,000,000 and not less than $2,000,000 per recipient.

children.heading: .—Focused research
children.label: ii .—Focused research
children.text: A consortium receiving a grant under this paragraph shall focus research on 1 of the transportation issue areas specified in section 6503(c).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: As a condition of receiving a grant under this paragraph, a grant recipient shall match 100 percent of the amounts made available under the grant.

children.label: I
children.text: ; orsection 504(b) of title 23

children.label: II
children.text: .section 505 of title 23

children.heading: .—Location of regional centers
children.label: A .—Location of regional centers
children.text: One regional university transportation center shall be located in each of the 10 Federal regions that comprise the Standard Federal Regions established by the Office of Management and Budget in the document entitled “Standard Federal Regions” and dated April 1974 (circular A–105).

children.label: i
children.text: the criteria described in subsection (b)(4);

children.label: ii
children.text: the location of the lead center within the Federal region to be served; and

children.label: I
children.text: recent expenditures by the institution in highway or public transportation research;

children.label: II
children.text: a historical track record of awarding graduate degrees in professional fields closely related to highways and public transportation; and

children.label: III
children.text: an experienced faculty who specialize in professional fields closely related to highways and public transportation.

children.heading: .—Restrictions
children.label: C .—Restrictions
children.text: For each fiscal year, a grant made available under this paragraph shall be not greater than $3,000,000 and not less than $1,500,000 per recipient.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: As a condition of receiving a grant under this paragraph, a grant recipient shall match 100 percent of the amounts made available under the grant.

children.label: I
children.text: ; orsection 504(b) of title 23

children.label: II
children.text: .section 505 of title 23

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: A regional university transportation center receiving a grant under this paragraph shall carry out research focusing on 1 or more of the matters described in subparagraphs (A) through (G) of section 6503(c)(1).

children.heading: .—Focused objectives
children.label: ii .—Focused objectives
children.text: The Secretary shall make a grant to 1 of the 10 regional university transportation centers established under this paragraph for the purpose of furthering the objectives described in subsection (a)(2) in the field of comprehensive transportation safety, congestion, connected vehicles, connected infrastructure, and autonomous vehicles, including the cybersecurity implications of technologies relating to connected vehicles, connected infrastructure, and autonomous vehicles.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall provide grants of not greater than $2,000,000 and not less than $1,000,000 to not more than 20 recipients to carry out this paragraph.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: As a condition of receiving a grant under this paragraph, a grant recipient shall match 50 percent of the amounts made available under the grant.

children.label: I
children.text: ; orsection 504(b) of title 23

children.label: II
children.text: .section 505 of title 23

children.heading: .—Focused research
children.label: C .—Focused research
children.text: In awarding grants under this section, consideration shall be given to minority institutions, as defined by section 365 of the Higher Education Act of 1965 (), or consortia that include such institutions that have demonstrated an ability in transportation-related research.20 U.S.C. 1067k

children.label: A
children.text: coordinate the research, education, and technology transfer activities carried out by grant recipients under this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: disseminate the results of that research through the establishment and operation of a publicly accessible online information clearinghouse.

children.label: A
children.text: review and evaluate the programs carried out under this section by grant recipients; and

children.label: B
children.text: make available to the public on a website of the Department of Transportation a report describing that review and evaluation.

children.heading: .—Program evaluation and oversight
children.label: 3 .—Program evaluation and oversight
children.text: For each of fiscal years 2022 through 2026, the Secretary shall expend not more than 1 and a half percent of the amounts made available to the Secretary to carry out this section for any coordination, evaluation, and oversight activities of the Secretary under this section.

children.heading: .—Limitation on Availability of Amounts
children.label: e .—Limitation on Availability of Amounts
children.text: Amounts made available to the Secretary to carry out this section shall remain available for obligation by the Secretary for a period of 3 years after the last day of the fiscal year for which the amounts are authorized.

children.heading: .—Information Collection
children.label: f .—Information Collection
children.text: Any survey, questionnaire, or interview that the Secretary determines to be necessary to carry out reporting requirements relating to any program assessment or evaluation activity under this section, including customer satisfaction assessments, shall not be subject to chapter 35 of title 44.
